articles.title: Polymarket opens pop-up bar for in person bets - but its first night was a bust
articles.summary: Polymarket launched a physical pop-up bar where people could place bets in person, but the debut night flopped with poor turnout. The failure highlights how prediction markets still struggle to bridge the gap between their online crypto-native audience and mainstream retail interest.

articles.title: Opinion | Trump is right about polls — and the better bet
articles.summary: Trump's calling out polling accuracy again, and this time someone thinks he's onto something about systematic polling errors or methodology flaws. If polls are consistently missing the mark, smart money will keep flowing toward betting markets as the more reliable gauge of actual electoral sentiment.

articles.title: Congress introduces bills to restrict prediction markets
articles.summary: Congress is moving to clamp down on prediction markets with new restrictive legislation. The bills could kill the recent momentum that's made platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket household names, potentially forcing the industry back underground or overseas.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets Are Changing the Music Industry
articles.summary: Music labels and streaming platforms are starting to use prediction markets to forecast which songs will hit, which artists will break through, and how album releases will perform. Smart money is realizing that fan sentiment and crowd wisdom often beat A&R executives at spotting the next big thing, creating new opportunities for bettors who understand music trends better than industry insiders.

articles.title: Sens. Schiff and Curtis on the Prediction Markets Are Gambling Act, state of DHS negotiations
articles.summary: Senators Adam Schiff and John Curtis are discussing their proposed Prediction Markets Are Gambling Act and ongoing negotiations with the Department of Homeland Security about regulating prediction markets. The bill could reshape how platforms like Kalshi and PredictIt operate, potentially forcing them to comply with gambling laws that would severely limit their scope and accessibility.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic won't return to normal for months, Kalshi bettors predict
articles.summary: Kalshi bettors are wagering that disrupted tanker traffic through the critical Strait of Hormuz will take months to normalize, suggesting ongoing geopolitical tensions or infrastructure issues in the world's most important oil chokepoint. The market signal points to sustained energy price volatility and supply chain disruptions that smart money expects to persist far longer than headlines suggest.

articles.title: Exclusive: Trader made nearly $1 million on Polymarket with remarkably accurate Iran bets
articles.summary: A Polymarket trader turned geopolitical analysis into serious cash, banking nearly $1M by correctly predicting Iran-related events with unusual precision. This shows how deep regional expertise can create massive edges in political prediction markets, especially when most bettors rely on headlines instead of real intelligence.

articles.title: Analysis | Taking a gamble on prediction markets
articles.summary: Someone's analyzing whether prediction markets are worth the risk, probably weighing their accuracy against traditional polling or expert forecasts. If mainstream outlets are seriously debating prediction markets as legitimate information sources, we're getting closer to real institutional adoption and bigger money flowing in.

articles.title: Lawmakers Introduce Bipartisan Bill Banning Sports Bets on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Congress wants to stop people from betting on sports through prediction markets like Kalshi and PredictIt. The bill would force platforms to choose between offering political predictions or sports bets, likely pushing them toward politics where the real money and attention live anyway.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Coming-Out Party in Washington Was a Disaster
articles.summary: Polymarket's first major Washington DC event to court regulators and legitimize prediction markets went sideways, likely featuring awkward exchanges between crypto-betting executives and skeptical policymakers. The fumbled debut signals that regulatory acceptance remains elusive, which could keep institutional money on the sidelines and force serious bettors to stick with overseas platforms.
